name: Alfheim
description: Alfheim, meaning "Elf Home" or "Elf World," is one of the Nine Worlds in Norse cosmology and is the homeland of the Light Elves (Ljósálfar). It is often described as a realm of radiant beauty, filled with shimmering light and natural splendor. Ruled by the god Freyr, Alfheim is a place of peace, joy, and artistic inspiration. The Light Elves are known for their wisdom, magical abilities, and connection to the natural world. They are considered benevolent beings who inspire creativity and bring good fortune to those who are worthy. Alfheim stands in contrast to Svartalfheim, the home of the Dark Elves, representing the duality of light and darkness, good and evil, within the Norse worldview. Its existence highlights the importance of balance and the appreciation of beauty in the cosmos. The realm is said to be located near Asgard, further emphasizing its connection to the divine and the importance of its inhabitants in the overall cosmic order. It is a testament to the Norse appreciation for the beauty and magic found in the natural world.
subtitle: Home of the Light Elves

keyMyths.title: Freyr's Rule
keyMyths.description: Freyr was given Alfheim as a tooth-fee when he was a child, signifying his dominion over the realm and its inhabitants. This established him as the lord of the Light Elves and solidified Alfheim's place in the divine order.

keyMyths.title: Light Elves' Influence
keyMyths.description: The Light Elves are believed to inspire artistry and bring good fortune to humans, influencing creativity and prosperity. Their connection to nature makes them a vital part of the Norse understanding of the world's balance.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for understanding Norse mythology, including descriptions of Alfheim and its inhabitants.
sources.text: A key source for understanding Norse mythology, including descriptions of Alfheim and its inhabitants.

sources.author: Various
